steps.title: Set up the weight-loss equation
steps.content:
The bear starts at 356 pounds and loses 0.7 pounds per day. After $d$ days, the weight is the initial weight minus the total loss:
$$321 = 356 - 0.7d$$

steps.title: Solve for the number of days
steps.content:
Rearrange to isolate $0.7d$:
$$0.7d = 356 - 321 = 35$$
Divide both sides by 0.7:
$$d = \frac{35}{0.7} = 50$$
The bear weighed 321 pounds after 50 days.
